title: Airfoil Analysis Benchmark
description: Compare two airfoils across low-fidelity and higher-fidelity methods, then explain where the predictions agree, drift, or break down.
whyItMatters: Aerospace engineers constantly choose between speed and fidelity. This project teaches how to use panel methods and CFD responsibly rather than treating any single tool output as truth.

steps.title: Choose the airfoils and flight point
steps.description: Select two airfoils for the same mission and define Reynolds number, Mach number, and angle-of-attack range.
steps.tips: A meaningful comparison starts with a common mission context.

steps.title: Run a fast panel-method study
steps.description: Generate lift and drag polars using a tool like XFLR5 or another low-order solver.
steps.tips: Document assumptions such as transition location and inviscid limitations.

steps.title: Run a higher-fidelity case
steps.description: Set up a CFD or validated higher-order analysis for a smaller subset of conditions and compare the results.
steps.tips: Use one carefully checked CFD case instead of many rushed ones.

steps.title: Identify the divergence
steps.description: Explain where the methods separate and what physics or modelling limits are causing the gap.
steps.tips: Look for stall onset, compressibility, or boundary-layer effects.

steps.title: Recommend an airfoil
steps.description: Write a short recommendation for which airfoil you would carry into preliminary design and why.
steps.tips: The best engineering answer includes uncertainty, not just a winner.

deliverables: Comparison table of airfoil geometry and design intent
Lift and drag polars from two analysis methods
Short note on modelling assumptions and limits
Recommendation memo for the preferred airfoil

rubric.description: Flow conditions, geometry, and assumptions are chosen and documented clearly.
The work explains why the methods agree or disagree rather than only plotting curves.
The recommendation reflects mission needs and modelling uncertainty.
Plots are labelled well and the takeaways are easy to follow.
